_meta.description: Hand-labeled ground truth of REAL target sessions, reconstructed by close-reading 871 real messages backfilled from the 3 production channels (2026-07-05 .. 2026-07-09), rule-parser only (no LLM). Built to evaluate track_eval.py's session/track accuracy against what the pipeline actually produces. Each session's message_keys are [source_name, telegram_message_id] pairs — the STABLE identity, since raw_messages.id is a local autoincrement PK that gets reassigned every time eval_backfill.db is rebuilt from a fresh Telegram fetch. Rebuild the DB via `DATABASE_URL=sqlite+aiosqlite:///./eval_backfill.db TELEGRAM_BACKFILL=300 LLM_FALLBACK_ENABLED=false .venv/bin/python eval/backfill_once.py` (safe to rerun — message_keys survive it); if the channels have posted 300+ new messages since 2026-07-09 some of the oldest labeled messages may fall out of the window.
_meta.known_cross_chunk_merge: d8a_s13 (cut off at the 07-08 morning/afternoon chunk boundary, still tracking a Bryansk-launched pair approaching Slavutych) and d8b_s0_slavutych_pair (afternoon chunk opens mid-flight with 'two targets circling near Slavutych') are almost certainly the same real target session split by the chunking boundary — merged into one session below (d8_s13_merged) rather than left as two.
_meta.built: 2026-07-09, via 6 parallel close-reading agents (one per day, 07-08 split into two halves)

sessions.note: merged across the 07-08 morning/afternoon chunk boundary (d8a_s13 + d8b_s0_slavutych_pair)
